class MinHeap
    def initialize(&comparator)
        @heap = []
        @comparator = comparator || ->(a, b) { a[0] <=> b[0] } # Default comparator for arrays
    end

    def size
        @heap.length
    end

    def empty?
        @heap.empty?
    end

    def peek
        @heap[0]
    end

    def push(item)
        @heap.push(item)
        bubble_up(@heap.length - 1)
    end

    def pop
        return nil if empty?
        top = @heap[0]
        last = @heap.pop
        if !empty?
            @heap[0] = last
            bubble_down(0)
        end
        top
    end

    private

    def bubble_up(index)
        while index > 0
            parent_index = (index - 1) / 2
            # If current item is prioritized over parent
            if @comparator[@heap[index], @heap[parent_index]] < 0
                @heap[index], @heap[parent_index] = @heap[parent_index], @heap[index]
                index = parent_index
            else
                break
            end
        end
    end

    def bubble_down(index)
        n = @heap.length
        while true
            left_child_index = 2 * index + 1
            right_child_index = 2 * index + 2
            smallest = index

            # Compare with left child
            if left_child_index < n && @comparator[@heap[left_child_index], @heap[smallest]] < 0
                smallest = left_child_index
            end

            # Compare with right child
            if right_child_index < n && @comparator[@heap[right_child_index], @heap[smallest]] < 0
                smallest = right_child_index
            end

            if smallest != index
                @heap[index], @heap[smallest] = @heap[smallest], @heap[index]
                index = smallest
            else
                break
            end
        end
    end
end

def solve(n, m, sources)
    R = n
    C = m

    # Initialize distance array (Time) to Infinity
    # Use a large number instead of true Infinity for robust comparison if needed, but Float::INFINITY works.
    dist = Array.new(R) { Array.new(C, Float::INFINITY) }
    
    # Initialize color grid to 0 (uncolored)
    final_grid = Array.new(R) { Array.new(C, 0) }

    # Priority Queue setup for Dijkstra.
    # State in PQ: [Time, NegativeColor, Row, Col]
    # Comparator prioritizes:
    # 1. Minimum Time (Time)
    # 2. Maximum Color (Minimum NegativeColor)
    
    # Comparator: a[0] vs b[0] (Time) -> Min Time first. 
    # If equal, a[1] vs b[1] (NegColor) -> Min NegColor first (Max Color).
    pq = MinHeap.new do |a, b|
        if a[0] != b[0]
            a[0] <=> b[0] # Min Time
        else
            a[1] <=> b[1] # Min NegativeColor (Max Color)
        end
    end

    # Initialize sources
    sources.each do |source|
        r, c, color = source
        
        # Since constraints guarantee unique starting positions, we just initialize.
        if dist[r][c] > 0
            dist[r][c] = 0
            final_grid[r][c] = color
            # Push [Time=0, NegativeColor=-color, R, C]
            pq.push([0, -color, r, c])
        end
    end

    directions = [[0, 1], [0, -1], [1, 0], [-1, 0]]

    while !pq.empty?
        state = pq.pop
        time_curr, neg_color_curr, r, c = state
        color_curr = -neg_color_curr

        # Stale check: If we found a faster path already, ignore this entry.
        if time_curr > dist[r][c]
            next
        end
        
        # Note: We don't need to explicitly check color here because if a state 
        # with a better color arrived at the same time, it would have been prioritized 
        # earlier or would be currently processed. The logic relies on relaxation updates.

        for dr, dc in directions
            nr = r + dr
            nc = c + dc

            # Check boundaries
            next unless nr >= 0 && nr < R && nc >= 0 && nc < C
            
            time_new = time_curr + 1

            # Case 1: Found a strictly faster path (Minimum Time update)
            if time_new < dist[nr][nc]
                dist[nr][nc] = time_new
                final_grid[nr][nc] = color_curr
                pq.push([time_new, -color_curr, nr, nc])
            # Case 2: Tie in arrival time (Color update)
            elsif time_new == dist[nr][nc]
                # Check for better color in case of time tie
                if color_curr > final_grid[nr][nc]
                    final_grid[nr][nc] = color_curr
                    # Must re-push this state to propagate the better color outwards
                    pq.push([time_new, -color_curr, nr, nc])
                end
            # Case 3: Slower path, do nothing.
            end
        end
    end

    return final_grid
end

        last_index = @heap.length - 1
        @heap[0] = @heap.pop

        bubble_down(0)
        
        return top
    end

    def bubble_up(index)
        while index > 0
            parent_index = (index - 1) / 2
            # If current item has higher priority than parent (is "smaller")
